 {"id":"bd-j9jo3.9","title":"robot-redesign/validation: tests, docs, transition, and legacy cleanup","description":"Add the deterministic fixtures, unit tests, schema goldens, integration coverage, Go-based E2E drivers, benchmark regressions, documentation refresh, transition notes, and legacy-path removal needed to make the redesign durable instead of aspirational. This epic exists because contract-heavy work only becomes real when behavior is pinned down, debugging is practical, cost regressions are visible, and the old assembly paths are retired safely.","design":"The redesign will touch contracts, SQLite persistence, event replay, explainability, renderers, and multiple transports. That means we need: checked-in example payloads and scenario matrices; shared deterministic Go test harnesses and fault injectors under testdata; package-local _test.go unit suites; integration coverage for persistence, degraded recovery, restart safety, and diagnostic evidence flow; Go-based E2E drivers under e2e/ that exercise CLI, REST, and streaming parity with verbose logging of commands, cursors, schema IDs, source_health, incidents, explainability handles, and assertion diffs; benchmark and regression coverage for payload size, latency, diagnostics cost, and storage churn; and explicit cleanup of superseded paths.

This epic is where we force the new architecture to become the only architecture, rather than one more parallel layer.","acceptance_criteria":"This epic is complete when deterministic Go-based unit, integration, benchmark, and E2E coverage pin the redesign's contract, ergonomics, explainability, and cost profile with great detailed logging; repository docs are self-contained and point to the example corpus and test entry points; and legacy robot assembly paths have been removed so the shared projection architecture is the only supported path.","notes":"Do not declare success early just because the new snapshot output looks good once. The bar is parity, restart safety, contract stability, comprehensive automated verification, and removal of redundant logic. Validation beads are part of the architecture, not optional QA garnish.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-21T23:32:13.892254319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T00:12:54.073288880Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robot-redesign","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:32:13.892254319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":321,"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Future self: the cleanup work is part of the feature, not optional janitorial follow-up.","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:32:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","title":"robot-redesign/validation: add schema goldens and contract stability tests","description":"Add golden tests and contract stability checks for robot payload schemas, checked-in example payloads, and registry-backed discovery output. This task exists because the redesign only improves long-term quality if contract drift becomes obvious and explainable.","design":"The tests should pin checked-in schema artifacts, normative example payloads and scenario transcripts, registry-generated capabilities/help/schema views, representative payload shapes for core surfaces, canonical error or remediation examples for invalid cursor, resync-required, bounded-result, duplicate-request, and invalid-inspect conditions, request identity or idempotency examples, correlated actuation outcomes, operator-attention-state examples such as acknowledge or snooze or pin transitions, and diagnostic explainability examples such as explanation-handle shapes, evidence summaries, suppression markers, and why-this-is-stale or truncated flows. They should make breaking changes explicit, keep additive changes disciplined, and log schema IDs, example names, changed sections, error codes, request or outcome IDs, operator-state markers, diagnostic handles, and high-signal diffs when goldens move. The focus is not on testing every field serializer in isolation; it is on safeguarding the declared contract and its concrete examples.","acceptance_criteria":"This task is complete when schema, example, and discovery drift trigger obvious test failures with rich diagnostics, and maintainers can evolve the robot contract only through explicit intentional updates to schemas and examples, including retry-safe, attention-hygiene, and operator-override semantics.","notes":"Use the example corpus as an executable contract, not as a bag of arbitrary snapshots.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T23:38:26.297471362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:43:01.378318402Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:43:01.377959981Z","close_reason":"Implemented schema goldens and contract stability tests:

Golden files created:
- 9 schema goldens (status, snapshot, attention, digest, 5 inspect surfaces)
- Registry surfaces golden (135 surfaces)
- 7 canonical error response goldens

Contract stability tests added:
- TestGolden_CoreSchemas, TestGolden_Registry, TestGolden_ErrorResponses, TestGolden_ScenarioPayloads
- TestContract_RequiredSchemaFields, TestContract_ErrorCodeStability
- TestContract_RegistrySurfaceStability, TestContract_IdempotencySupport

Usage: Run with -update-goldens to regenerate when contracts change.

 {"id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","title":"robot-redesign/validation: add benchmark and regression coverage for latency, payload size, and storage churn","description":"Add automated benchmark and regression coverage for the user-facing cost profile of the redesign. This task exists because robot mode ergonomics depend not only on semantic correctness but also on snapshot, status, digest, attention, inspect, and transport behavior staying fast, bounded, and operationally cheap as projects grow.","design":"The coverage should use the shared deterministic fixture corpus and Go testing infrastructure to measure snapshot, status, digest, attention, inspect, diagnostics, and renderer behavior under small, medium, and large scenarios plus repeated-event, retry-heavy, and operator-override-heavy scenarios. It should track payload bytes, section counts, raw event counts versus surfaced item counts where observable, truncation or pagination activation, latency, allocations, rows touched or rewritten where observable, replay throughput, diagnostic expansion cost, deduplication effectiveness, operator-state impact on surfaced queue size, and any storage-churn signals needed to verify SQLite stays operationally cheap. Use Go benchmarks and targeted regression tests rather than ad hoc shell loops. The logging should record scenario IDs, schema IDs, request IDs where relevant, render format, item counts, payload sizes, operator-state markers, suppression or resurfacing markers, diagnostic-surface depth where relevant, and enough supporting detail that a regression report explains what got slower or larger and why that matters to users.","acceptance_criteria":"This task is complete when the redesign has automated benchmark and regression coverage for latency, payload size, storage churn, compact-attention effectiveness, and operator-control overhead, with detailed logs that make regressions obvious and diagnosable. The result should prove that boundedness and performance budgets are being honored for representative scenarios rather than merely hoped for.","notes":"Fast, bounded outputs are part of the contract for agent-facing robot mode. Benchmark work should protect user experience and operational cost, not chase meaningless micro-optimizations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-21T23:55:45.991262114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T22:15:34.988200873Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T22:15:34.987512244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","performance","robot-redesign","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:29:57.571500782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:28.813873196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:29.449135271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:39.832167367Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:23:48.140664593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:30:09.314787309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.4.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T00:48:12.943189537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:26:11.351998992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:57:30.103760616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.10", -{"id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","title":"robot-redesign/validation: add redaction, sensitivity, and disclosure-control verification coverage","description":"Add dedicated verification for sensitivity, redaction, and disclosure-control semantics so ntm can prove richer robot data stay safe across persistence, replay, diagnostics, and transport boundaries.","design":"The tests should cover contract examples for visible versus preview_only versus redacted versus withheld payloads; rule-version and reason markers; safe-preview generation for pane output, mail snippets, diagnostic evidence, and replay snapshots; no-leak guarantees in SQLite projections, attention journals, incident replay caches, REST payloads, and streaming events; allowlisted-field behavior; and explicit diagnostic explanations for why content was transformed. Coverage should include package-local unit tests, SQLite and restart integration tests, Go-based e2e scenarios, and benchmark or regression checks for redaction cost and payload-size effects. Logging should be excellent: scenario name, schema ID, rule version, disclosure state, hidden-field counts, hashed evidence handles, replay mode, and diffs that prove raw secrets were not emitted.","acceptance_criteria":"This task is complete when the redesign has explicit automated coverage proving that sensitive material is transformed or withheld consistently across live surfaces, replay, persistence, diagnostics, and parity transports, and when failures produce enough detailed logging that a maintainer can understand exactly what leaked, what was expected, and which rule or surface misbehaved.","notes":"Sensitivity handling is too important to leave as an implied side effect of generic tests. If a regression could leak a secret without a dedicated failing test and clear artifact trail, this task is incomplete.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-22T01:34:08.838581790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T22:19:28.720893056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robot-redesign","sensitivity","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:40.673125135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:41.531532027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:42.403226219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:43.306247021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:44.270220056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:07:55.154188545Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-26T19:45:16.279676807Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:36:45.281933126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:34:08.838581790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-j9jo3.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at"
